comment.body: #codex-reply Verified on device (Android 15, 1080×2340) by logging the applied padding and the content child's on-screen position. On Theme.AppCompat (backAndForth): padding=[269,45], content top at y=269, with the action bar occupying y=101–269 — ActionBarOverlayLayout insets the action bar itself and passes the content an augmented top inset (101 status + 168 action bar) that the content is expected to apply, which is exactly what this listener does; there is no double-inset, and without the listener the content would not be inset at all. On NoActionBar (comprehensiveDemo): padding=[101,45], content top at y=101, height 2194 = 2340−101−45. The call-order concern has no behavioral effect because AppCompatActivity.findViewById routes through AppCompatDelegateImpl.findViewById, which calls ensureSubDecor() — so android.R.id.content resolves to AppCompat's ContentFrameLayout in both orderings and is never null. Separately, rosbridgeTest and basicServer do not extend AbcvlibActivity (they extend AppCompatActivity and Activity), so they get no edge-to-edge handling and are visibly clipped — real, but outside this PR's declared scope and worth a follow-up issue.
